summary_en: TIEMCO's board opposes an unsolicited tender offer by Kenka Synergy Investment Partnership at ¥1,900/share, citing lack of prior disclosure and insufficient information on acquisition rationale and valuation basis.
title_en: Notice of Opposition Opinion Regarding Tender Offer for Company Stock by Kenka Synergy Investment Business Limited Liability Partnership
translation_en_brief:
On May 19, 2026, TIEMCO Limited (TSE Standard: 7501) announced that its board of directors has adopted a resolution to oppose an unsolicited tender offer by Kenka Synergy Investment Business Limited Liability Partnership for all outstanding common shares at ¥1,900 per share. The offer was unilaterally announced on April 7, 2026, without prior disclosure or consultation with the company.

The company had initially expressed a reserved opinion on April 21, 2026, while conducting due diligence with independent legal counsel (Tokyo International Law Office). The board's opposition is grounded in material information gaps: the bidder has not adequately disclosed the acquisition's strategic purpose, the basis for the ¥1,900 valuation, post-acquisition management intentions, or how the transaction would affect corporate value and shareholder interests.

To ensure procedural fairness and transparency, the board established a special committee comprising two independent audit committee directors and an external M&A specialist (certified public accountant) to evaluate the legitimacy of the bidder's intentions, the fairness of offer terms, transaction process integrity, and whether the board's eventual recommendation serves shareholders' interests. The company intends to use formal questioning mechanisms under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act to obtain critical information from the bidder.
